description: Return a list of all Timecard Entries, serialized according to the MyTime field set

parameters.name: filters[id]
parameters.description: Return item(s) with the specified IDs.

parameters.name: filters[party_id]
parameters.description: Return item(s) with the specified Party ID.

parameters.name: filters[include_in_progress]
parameters.description: Return available and work in progress item(s).

parameters.name: filters[updated_at]
parameters.description:
Return item(s) last updated within the specified ISO 8601 datetime range.
Formats:
`YYYY-MM-DD`...`YYYY-MM-DD` - Date
`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`...`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ` - DateTime with UTC Offset
`YYY...

parameters.name: filters[deleted_at]
parameters.description: Return item(s) that were deleted within the specified ISO 8601 datetime range.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: The beginning of the date range for entries. (YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: The ending of the date range for entries. (YYYY-MM-DD)

parameters.name: start_time_in
parameters.description: The beginning of the time_in range for entries (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). "Z" represents the timezone offset (i.e. -08:00, -0800). Optionally you may pass the literal "Z" which also means "UTC".

parameters.name: end_time_in
parameters.description: The ending of the time_in range for entries (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). "Z" represents the timezone offset (i.e. -08:00, -0800). Optionally you may pass the literal "Z" which also means "UTC".

parameters.name: use_filter_tz
parameters.description: When passed as "true" the timezone from start_time_in or end_time_in will be used for all timestamps in the response. Otherwise they'll use UTC.

parameters.name: serializer_view
parameters.description: Changes what fields are included in the response.

schema.description: Array of timecard entries.  NOTE the timecards entries will be serialized according to the MyTime field set, not the Timesheet field set.
